title: pinus kesiya subsp. insularis
intro: Pinus kesiya subsp. insularis, also known as the Himalayan white pine, is a subspecies of the Himalayan white pine tree that is native to the Himalayan region. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which can be utilized to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Pinus kesiya subsp. insularis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and support immune function. In terms of herbal preparations, the leaves and needles of this plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
